posts.author: mohitmehra
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — Saw a bunch of questions around this on twitter, so had put this out. x.com Mohit Mehra (@mohitmehra) on X @mohitmehra NCDEX is launching weather derivatives for Mumbai rains. Isn't this gambling? Is this a slippery slope? What gets traded next if even rainfall does? It's a fair instinct, but the contract isn't arbitrary. Weather has been informally hedged in India for years, but this version NCDEX is launching weather derivatives for Mumbai rains. Isn’t this gambling? Is this a slippery slope? What gets traded next if even rainfall does? It’s a fair instinct,…

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — Sharks will now manipulate weather

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — They can though. If the derivative market is big enough, things like cloud seeding can be attempted to force a preferred outcome for the whales, but greasing the hands of few meteorological survey agents would be much easier.

posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — Typo here: It is losing and not loosing. let me be that person, who pointed this out to you, so you remember me the next time you correct yourself on this.

posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — If the outcome cannot be manipulated by artificial demand or supply then retailers will have a far better chance of being profitable on weather options trade. Interesting.

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: Weather Derivatives hit Markets — IMG_4477 1179×626 69.8 KB Back in some days SEBI super concerned about retailers loosing in options, just to prevent them so much buzz were there. Now they sliding weather derivatives & everyone is quiet. As a legend said: “Hypocrisy ki bhi Sima hoti hai”
